senses.definition: to take action based on one's words or beliefs and living up to the standards that one talks about

examples.x: It's not enough to talk about environmental conservation; you need to walk the walk by reducing your own carbon footprint.
Leaders should not only talk about integrity but also walk the walk by demonstrating ethical behavior in their actions.
She doesn't just talk about the importance of volunteering; she actively walks the walk by dedicating her time to community service.
A true advocate for equality doesn't just talk the talk; they also walk the walk by actively promoting inclusivity in their daily life.
To be an effective mentor, you need to not only talk the talk but also walk the walk by setting a positive example for your mentees.

senses.definition: to make a firm promise to someone

examples.x: He gave his word that he would be there for the meeting, so we can rely on him to attend.
She gave her word to keep the secret, assuring her friend that she would not disclose the information to anyone else.
The politician gave his word to address the concerns of the community and work towards finding a solution.
They gave their word to complete the project by the deadline, instilling confidence in the client that it would be delivered on time.
The witness gave their word to tell the truth during the trial, pledging to provide accurate and honest testimony.

senses.definition: to believe what someone says without needing further proof

examples.x: He claimed to have seen a UFO, and without any other evidence, I had to take his word for it.
She said the book was excellent, so I decided to take her word for it and purchased a copy.
The mechanic assured me that the repairs were done correctly, and I had to take their word for it since I had no knowledge of car mechanics.
My friend said the movie was incredibly funny, and I decided to take his word for it and went to see it.
The tour guide mentioned that the hike was challenging but rewarding, and we took their word for it and embarked on the adventure.

senses.definition: an assumption based on trust given to a person or thing, even though one is not entirely sure

examples.x: Although his explanation seemed unlikely, I decided to give him the benefit of the doubt until more facts emerged.
She arrived late to the meeting, but I gave her the benefit of the doubt, assuming there was a valid reason for the delay.
The teacher gave the student the benefit of the doubt, allowing them to retake the test due to unforeseen circumstances.
Despite the suspicious circumstances, the jury gave the defendant the benefit of the doubt, acquitting them due to insufficient evidence.
I didn't receive a response to my email, but I'll give them the benefit of the doubt and assume they haven't seen it yet.

word_list.pronunciation: fɹʌm ðə bˈɑːɾəm ʌv wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt

senses.definition: in an honest or sincere manner

examples.x: The actor delivered a powerful speech, speaking from his heart and moving the audience with his authenticity and emotional connection.
She thanked him from the bottom of her heart for his kind gesture and support during a difficult time.
Her words of encouragement came straight from the bottom of her heart, inspiring others with her genuine belief in their abilities.
The apology was heartfelt, as he spoke from his heart, expressing true remorse for his actions.

senses.definition: to not think carefully before saying something or reacting to a situation

examples.x: He tends to shoot from the hip during debates, often expressing his opinions without considering the consequences.
The CEO's decision to launch the new product was a classic example of shooting from the hip, as it was made without thoroughly evaluating market demand.
She always shoots from the hip when giving feedback, providing honest and direct criticism, even if it may be uncomfortable to hear.
His impulsive decision to quit his job and start a business was a result of shooting from the hip, driven by a desire for change without a detailed plan.
The politician's controversial remarks during the interview showed his tendency to shoot from the hip, often making provocative statements without considering their impact.

senses.definition: to not hesitate to do or say what one truly wants

examples.x: He made no bones about his dislike for spicy food and refused to eat anything with even a hint of spice.
She made no bones about her ambition, openly expressing her desire to become the CEO of the company.
The manager made no bones about the tough decisions that needed to be made in order to save the company from bankruptcy.
He made no bones about his disappointment, openly expressing his frustration with the team's performance.
The professor made no bones about the difficulty of the upcoming exam, warning the students to prepare thoroughly.

senses.definition: to talk about something openly and frankly, particularly something that is unpleasant to hear

examples.x: In the meeting, he decided to lay it on the line and express his concerns about the project's feasibility.
She knew she had to lay it on the line and tell her friend the truth, even though it might hurt their feelings.
The employee decided to lay it on the line with his boss and discuss the issues affecting his job satisfaction.
The coach gathered the team to lay it on the line and motivate them to give their best performance in the upcoming game.
The therapist encouraged her patient to lay it on the line and share their innermost thoughts and emotions to make progress in therapy.

senses.definition: to directly tell a person a fact, usually one that is unpleasant

examples.x: I appreciate when my friends give it to me straight and offer their honest opinions, even if it's not what I want to hear.

senses.definition: to seriously and honestly discuss a matter

examples.x: During the contract negotiation, the parties sat down to talk turkey and hammered out the specific terms and conditions.
The manager called the team together to talk turkey about the upcoming project and assign responsibilities.
Instead of beating around the bush, let's talk turkey and address the challenges we are facing head-on.
The salesperson was known for her ability to talk turkey with clients, understanding their needs and providing tailored solutions.
The mediator encouraged the disputing parties to talk turkey and find common ground to resolve the conflict.

senses.definition: to show one's criticism or disapproval without considering other people's feelings

examples.x: My father is very direct and pulls no punches.
In the review, the manager pulled no punches when discussing the employee's underperformance.

senses.definition: in a completely honest and frank way, particularly used for a discussion between two men

examples.x: As friends, they knew they could always speak openly, man to man, about anything troubling them.
Despite their differences, the diplomats knew they needed to negotiate man to man to reach a peaceful resolution.
The two soldiers had a heart-to-heart talk, man to man, about the challenges they faced in the warzone.
In times of conflict, it's essential to address issues directly, man to man, without evasion or deflection.

senses.definition: (of a fact stated) in a way that is very direct and clear

examples.x: She didn't explicitly say it, but in so many words, she implied that she was not happy with the decision.
He didn't directly admit his mistake, but in as many words, he acknowledged his error and apologized for it.
The teacher didn't explain the concept thoroughly, but in so many words, she conveyed the main idea to the students.
He didn't want to hurt her feelings, so he hinted at his opinion instead of saying it in so many words.
The politician didn't answer the question directly, but in as many words, he conveyed his stance on the issue.

senses.definition: an agreement that is based on the mutual trust of the parties, which is of no legal value

examples.x: The business partners reached a gentleman's agreement to split the profits equally, even though it was not officially documented.
The neighbors had a gentleman's agreement to take turns mowing the shared lawn, without the need for a formal schedule.
The football clubs had a gentleman's agreement not to approach each other's star players during the transfer window.
The two authors had a gentleman's agreement to acknowledge each other's contributions in their respective books, despite not specifying it in writing.
The diplomats reached a gentleman's agreement on the disputed border, based on trust and mutual understanding, rather than a legally binding treaty.

word_list.pronunciation: wˈɛɹ hæv wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt ˌɑːn wˈʌnz slˈiːv

senses.definition: to make no effort to hide one's true feelings and intentions

examples.x: She wears her heart on her sleeve and is always quick to show her excitement and joy.
He is not one to wear his heart on his sleeve, preferring to keep his emotions private.
The poet's verses reveal that he wears his heart on his sleeve, pouring his deepest emotions onto the page.
Despite his tough exterior, he wears his heart on his sleeve when it comes to his family, expressing his love and concern openly.
She wore her heart on her sleeve during the heartfelt speech, moving the audience with her sincerity and passion.

word_list.pronunciation: ˈoʊpən wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt tʊ ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to reveal to a person, an organization, etc. what one truly feels or thinks

examples.x: After years of secrecy, she finally opened her heart to her closest friend, sharing the pain she had been carrying.
He opened his heart to the idea of forgiveness and decided to give his estranged family member a second chance.
The children's book opened their hearts to the magic of imagination and sparked a lifelong love for reading.
He opened his heart to his partner, revealing his fears and insecurities, and they grew closer as a result.

senses.definition: (of a business) to share confidential or sensitive information to a business partner or colleague

examples.x: The CEO decided to open the kimono and share the company's financial challenges with the board of directors.
During the merger negotiations, both companies agreed to open the kimono and exchange detailed financial data.
The consultant requested that the client open the kimono and provide access to their internal processes for a thorough analysis.
The team leader urged the team members to open the kimono and share their ideas and concerns during the brainstorming session.
The government official promised to open the kimono and provide full transparency in addressing the public's concerns about the controversial policy.

senses.definition: to start to talk about what one truly feels or thinks

examples.x: In her memoir, the author bared her soul, recounting her personal struggles and triumphs with raw honesty.
After years of hiding his true self, he finally found the courage to bare his heart to his closest friends, embracing his authentic identity.
The therapy session provided a safe space for him to bare his soul, exploring the underlying issues that had been holding him back.
During the heartfelt conversation, she bared her heart to her partner, expressing her deepest fears, hopes, and dreams.
The artist's paintings were a way for her to bare her soul to the world, channeling her emotions and experiences onto the canvas.

senses.definition: to openly share one's thoughts and feelings with someone

examples.x: She sat with her best friend and poured her heart out, sharing the pain and sorrow she had been carrying for so long.
The singer poured her heart out on stage, delivering a powerful performance that moved the audience to tears.

senses.definition: to share with someone all one's feelings, most private thoughts, and secrets

examples.x: Despite his initial hesitation, John eventually decided to spill his guts out and share his deepest fears with his therapist.
It took a few glasses of wine, but eventually, Mark loosened up and started spilling his guts to his friends about his recent breakup.
Sarah and Jessica are best friends, and they always spill their guts to each other about everything.
